nuggets.headline: Did you know Radiohead launched one of their most celebrated albums with a controversial "pay-what-you-wish" model?
nuggets.text: The band made headlines with the 2007 release of *In Rainbows*, which included "Weird Fishes/Arpeggi," by initially offering it as a free internet download where fans could pay whatever they felt it was worth. This bold move came after years of the band's "dire warnings of the imposition and creeping dehumanisation the electronic world of machines would cause," making it a fascinating paradox.

source.title: Radiohead, In Rainbows. and how giving away a near perfect… | by Stephen Blackford | Medium
source.quoteSnippet: an album they at first gave away as a free/pay what you wish internet download and on the back of their decades long dire warnings of the imposition and creeping dehumanisation the electronic world of machines would cause.

nuggets.headline: The ethereal, "oceanic" sound of "Weird Fishes/Arpeggi" was first heard in an unexpected setting, years before the album's release.
nuggets.text: Before its release on *In Rainbows*, this intricate track, noted for its layered, multi-pattern guitar parts that create an immersive, oceanic soundscape, was actually premiered live. Thom Yorke and Jonny Greenwood first performed "Weird Fishes" with the Nazareth Orchestra at London's Ether Festival in March 2005, two years before the album's official release.

source.title: Weird Fishes / Arpeggi by Radiohead - Songfacts
source.quoteSnippet: Thom Yorke and Jonny Greenwood from Radiohead together with the Nazareth Orchestra premiered this song at the Ether Festival (Royal Festival Hall, London) on March 27, 2005.

nuggets.imageCaption: Radiohead performing live, showcasing their intricate musicianship.

nuggets.headline: If the intricate guitar work and emotional depth of "Weird Fishes/Arpeggi" resonated with you, dive into Jeff Buckley's iconic sound.
nuggets.text: Buckley's seminal album *Grace* showcases his incredible vocal range and sophisticated, often melancholic arrangements with complex guitar arpeggios that share a similar atmospheric and introspective quality. You'll especially appreciate tracks like "Grace" or "Hallelujah" for their emotional intensity and delicate musicianship, aligning with the alternative rock and distinctive vocal style found in "Weird Fishes/Arpeggi."

source.title: Thom Yorke: English musician and singer for Radiohead (1968-) | Biography, Facts, Information, Career, Wiki, Life
source.quoteSnippet: Yorke's early influences included alternative rock acts such as the Pixies and R.E.M. ... noted for his falsetto.

nuggets.imageCaption: Jeff Buckley, known for his emotive vocals and intricate guitar work.

artistSummary: Radiohead is an iconic English rock band known for their innovative blend of art rock and alternative sounds. Fronted by the distinctive Thom Yorke, they've released critically acclaimed albums like 'In Rainbows' and have sold over 30 million records, constantly pushing musical boundaries.
